speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Let them pull all about mine ears, present me

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Death on the wheel or at wild horses’ heels,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Or pile ten hills on the Tarpeian rock,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That the precipitation might down stretch

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Below the beam of sight, yet will I still

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Be thus to them.

speaker: NOBLE
text: You do the nobler.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I muse my mother

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Does not approve me further, who was wont

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To call them woolen vassals, things created

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To buy and sell with groats, to show bare heads

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: In congregations, to yawn, be still, and wonder

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: When one but of my ordinance stood up

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To speak of peace or war.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I talk of you.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Why did you wish me milder? Would you have me

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: False to my nature? Rather say I play

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The man I am.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: O sir, sir, sir,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: I would have had you put your power well on

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Before you had worn it out.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Let go.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: You might have been enough the man you are

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: With striving less to be so. Lesser had been

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: The thwartings of your dispositions if

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: You had not showed them how you were disposed

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Ere they lacked power to cross you.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Let them hang!

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Ay, and burn too.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Come, come, you have been too rough, something too rough.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: You must return and mend it.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: There’s no remedy,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Unless, by not so doing, our good city

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Cleave in the midst and perish.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Pray be counseled.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: I have a heart as little apt as yours,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: But yet a brain that leads my use of anger

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: To better vantage.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Well said, noble woman.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Before he should thus stoop to th’ herd—but that

speaker: MENENIUS
text: The violent fit o’ th’ time craves it as physic

speaker: MENENIUS
text: For the whole state—I would put mine armor on,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Which I can scarcely bear.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: What must I do?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Return to th’ Tribunes.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Well, what then? What then?

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Repent what you have spoke.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: For them? I cannot do it to the gods.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Must I then do ’t to them?

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: You are too absolute,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Though therein you can never be too noble

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: But when extremities speak. I have heard you say

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Honor and policy, like unsevered friends,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: I’ th’ war do grow together. Grant that, and tell me

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: In peace what each of them by th’ other lose

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: That they combine not there?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Tush, tush!

speaker: MENENIUS
text: A good demand.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: If it be honor in your wars to seem

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: The same you are not, which for your best ends

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: You adopt your policy, how is it less or worse

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: That it shall hold companionship in peace

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: With honor as in war, since that to both

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: It stands in like request?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Why force you this?

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Because that now it lies you on to speak

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: To th’ people, not by your own instruction,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Nor by th’ matter which your heart prompts you,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: But with such words that are but roted in

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Your tongue, though but bastards and syllables

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Of no allowance to your bosom’s truth.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Now, this no more dishonors you at all

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Than to take in a town with gentle words,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Which else would put you to your fortune and

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: The hazard of much blood.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: I would dissemble with my nature where

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: My fortunes and my friends at stake required

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: I should do so in honor. I am in this

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Your wife, your son, these senators, the nobles;

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: And you will rather show our general louts

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: How you can frown than spend a fawn upon ’em

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: For the inheritance of their loves and safeguard

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Of what that want might ruin.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Noble lady!—

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Come, go with us; speak fair. You may salve so,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Not what is dangerous present, but the loss

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Of what is past.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: I prithee now, my son,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Go to them with this bonnet in thy hand,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: And thus far having stretched it—here be with them—

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Thy knee bussing the stones—for in such business

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Action is eloquence, and the eyes of th’ ignorant

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: More learnèd than the ears—waving thy head,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Which often thus correcting thy stout heart,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Now humble as the ripest mulberry

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: That will not hold the handling. Or say to them

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Thou art their soldier and, being bred in broils,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Hast not the soft way, which thou dost confess

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Were fit for thee to use as they to claim,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: In asking their good loves; but thou wilt frame

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Thyself, forsooth, hereafter theirs, so far

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: As thou hast power and person.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: This but done

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Even as she speaks, why, their hearts were yours;

speaker: MENENIUS
text: For they have pardons, being asked, as free

speaker: MENENIUS
text: As words to little purpose.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Prithee now,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Go, and be ruled; although I know thou hadst rather

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Follow thine enemy in a fiery gulf

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Than flatter him in a bower.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Here is Cominius.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: I have been i’ th’ marketplace; and, sir, ’tis fit

speaker: COMINIUS
text: You make strong party or defend yourself

speaker: COMINIUS
text: By calmness or by absence. All’s in anger.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Only fair speech.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: I think ’twill serve, if he

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Can thereto frame his spirit.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: He must, and will.—

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Prithee, now, say you will, and go about it.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Must I go show them my unbarbèd sconce? Must I

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: With my base tongue give to my noble heart

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: A lie that it must bear? Well, I will do ’t.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Yet, were there but this single plot to lose,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: This mold of Martius, they to dust should grind it

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And throw ’t against the wind. To th’ marketplace!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: You have put me now to such a part which never

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I shall discharge to th’ life.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Come, come, we’ll prompt you.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: I prithee now, sweet son, as thou hast said

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: My praises made thee first a soldier, so,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: To have my praise for this, perform a part

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Thou hast not done before.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Well, I must do ’t.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Away, my disposition, and possess me

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Some harlot’s spirit! My throat of war be turned,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Which choirèd with my drum, into a pipe

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Small as an eunuch or the virgin voice

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That babies lull asleep! The smiles of knaves

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Tent in my cheeks, and schoolboys’ tears take up

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The glasses of my sight! A beggar’s tongue

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Make motion through my lips, and my armed knees,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Who bowed but in my stirrup, bend like his

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That hath received an alms. I will not do ’t,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Lest I surcease to honor mine own truth

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And, by my body’s action, teach my mind

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: A most inherent baseness.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: At thy choice, then.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: To beg of thee, it is my more dishonor

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Than thou of them. Come all to ruin. Let

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Thy mother rather feel thy pride than fear

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Thy dangerous stoutness, for I mock at death

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: With as big heart as thou. Do as thou list.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Thy valiantness was mine; thou suck’st it from me,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: But owe thy pride thyself.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Pray be content.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Mother, I am going to the marketplace.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Chide me no more. I’ll mountebank their loves,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Cog their hearts from them, and come home beloved

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Of all the trades in Rome. Look, I am going.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Commend me to my wife. I’ll return consul,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Or never trust to what my tongue can do

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I’ th’ way of flattery further.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Do your will.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Away! The Tribunes do attend you. Arm yourself

speaker: COMINIUS
text: To answer mildly, for they are prepared

speaker: COMINIUS
text: With accusations, as I hear, more strong

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Than are upon you yet.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The word is “mildly.” Pray you, let us go.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Let them accuse me by invention, I

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Will answer in mine honor.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Ay, but mildly.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Well, mildly be it, then. Mildly.
